lexical_units.contextual_meaning: military force or independence is represented through body, head, or sinews language
lexical_units.basic_meaning: an animal or human body, its head, and connective sinews
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Military-register uses may be conventional unit labels, but they also preserve body-politic or force-body imagery.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Military organization may be understood through bodily organization.
lexical_units.review_notes: Washington-document register caution: retain as uncertain rather than force a Lincoln-style body-politic reading.

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: military conduct is evaluated through glory, honor, or spirit language
lexical_units.basic_meaning: radiance/fame, public esteem, or animating breath/spirit
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The terms may be conventional military praise, but the source-domain contrast remains plausible in this register.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Military virtue is understood through honor, animation, and public radiance.
lexical_units.review_notes: Register-sensitive Washington decision: preserve uncertainty for later human review.
